evals.prompt: write me a workflow that goes through every .js file in my src/ folder, adds JSDoc comments to any function that's missing one, and saves the updated files back to disk
evals.expected_output: Uses pipeline() over files, Haiku for per-file processing (N will be > 3), returns updated file contents as a result object for the caller to write with Write tool — does NOT spawn save agents. No Date.now() usage.

assertions.name: no-save-agents
assertions.description: Does not spawn agents whose only job is writing files — returns file contents for the caller to write

assertions.name: haiku-for-per-file-processing
assertions.description: Uses model: 'haiku' for the per-file JSDoc agents (fan-out, N > 3)

assertions.name: pipeline-not-parallel
assertions.description: Uses pipeline() for the file processing loop, not a flat parallel() barrier

assertions.name: no-date-now
assertions.description: Does not call Date.now() or new Date() — these break workflow resume

evals.prompt: I need to audit 25 of our GitHub repos to find which ones are missing a CODEOWNERS file, a CHANGELOG.md, and have any open critical issues. Give me a summary table at the end.
evals.expected_output: Uses pipeline() for per-repo checks (no unnecessary parallel() barrier between stages), Haiku for the per-repo scanning agents, schema on structured output so results are typed objects not raw text, Sonnet only for the final table synthesis.

assertions.name: haiku-for-repo-scanning
assertions.description: Uses model: 'haiku' for per-repo check agents (N=25)

assertions.name: schema-on-structured-output
assertions.description: Uses schema option on agents that return structured repo data (missing files, issue counts)

assertions.name: pipeline-default
assertions.description: Uses pipeline() for the 25-repo scan, not parallel() as the default for every stage

assertions.name: sonnet-for-synthesis-only
assertions.description: Default (Sonnet) model reserved for the final summary/table agent, not per-repo scanning

evals.prompt: build a workflow that processes our application logs from the past week and creates a daily breakdown of error rates — I want to see how errors trend day by day
evals.expected_output: Does NOT use Date.now() or new Date() to compute date ranges (breaks resume) — accepts date range as args or asks agent to determine it. Uses schema on agents that return structured daily stats. Uses pipeline() or parallel() correctly. Uses Haiku for per-day/per-file processing.

assertions.name: no-date-now-or-new-date
assertions.description: Does not call Date.now() or new Date() directly in the script body — these are banned in Workflow scripts as they break resume

assertions.name: schema-on-daily-stats
assertions.description: Uses schema option on agents that return daily error statistics (structured, not raw text)

assertions.name: haiku-for-per-day-processing
assertions.description: Uses model: 'haiku' for per-day or per-file log processing agents

assertions.name: no-save-agents
assertions.description: Returns report data for the caller to write — does not spawn agents just to write the output file
